content.text: Bachelor’s, Master’s degree or PhD students and graduates, or industry professionals with experience in a related area.– Must be a passport holder from an EU Member State or one of the following associated countries: Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, EEA countries (Iceland, Norway, Liechtenstein), Kosovo, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, Türkiye, or Ukraine.– Requires certified evidence of English skills (unless a native speaker), specifically: IELTS score of 6.5TOEFL iBT score of 90.

content.heading: Tuition WaiverFee Waiver Valued at €5,500
content.text: Successful applicants will be eligible for a fee-waiver and a partial stipend of €1,000 per month, for eighteen (18) months or up until the end of the DigiWind project in December 2027. It is envisaged that up to five scholarships will be awarded. The successful candidates will be based at the TUS Campus in Limerick, Ireland studying in-person with access to online classes.

content.heading: Tuition WaiverFee Waiver Valued at €5,500
content.text: The Irish Design Engineering and Advanced Manufacturing (IDEAM) Research Institute, led by Dr John Cosgrove, is focused on the research and development of data-driven solutions for Industry. With core expertise in Engineering, Energy, Electronics, Informatics and Manufacturing, IDEAM is a comprehensive, multi-disciplinary institute focused on the development of excellence in research, collaborative research projects and regional training & skills that can deliver impactful research and innovation.The Research Institute has an extensive industry support base across Ireland, is currently collaborating on 24 industry-based research projects, and is directly supported by a strong ecosystem of 65 actively engaged companies who have contributed to the definition and growth of this research area.

content.heading: Discover why leading researchers and industry partners choose IDEAM Research Institute to advance innovation - from groundbreaking studies to global impact
content.text: The IDEAM Research Institute proudly showcases its impact through measurable achievements. With over €15 million in annual research funding, we drive transformative advancements in science and technology. Our 150+ impactful projects span diverse fields, collaborating with over 100 trusted industry partners and leading researchers.Through 65+ high-quality publications in renowned journals and conferences, we ensure our research contributes to global innovation and knowledge-sharing. Explore how our collaborative efforts and cutting-edge initiatives are shaping the future across industries.

content.heading: The Circular Economy Programme 2021-2027
content.text: This document outlines Ireland’s The Circular Economy Programme which will be led by the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA). The Circular Economy Programme incorporates and builds upon the previous National Waste Prevention Programme1 to support national-level, strategic programmes to prevent waste and drive the Circular Economy in Ireland. The development of the Circular Economy Programme responds to the commitment within the national policy document The Waste Action Plan for a Circular Economy to reconfigure the National Waste Prevention Programme into Ireland’s The Circular Economy Programme.
IDEAM InstituteMoylish CampusCo. LimerickV94 E8YF

title: IDEAM | Irish Digital Engineering And Advanced Manufacturing Cluster

content.text: IDEAM Cluster is an innovative national industry cluster located in the heart of the Mid-West region of Ireland supporting businesses to collaborate, innovate and grow. We aim to prepare those organisations for opportunities in digitalisation and green transition of manufacturing. The core needs of SMEs are at the heart of our cluster strategy acting as a ‘one-stop-shop’ to ignite the exponential growth of manufacturing SMEs. Our core vision is to unlock new collaborative opportunities for our members by working together and building trust.

content.heading: Explore What We Are Doing Better
content.text: Our Vision Mission and Ambition
*VAT is applicable to Membership
**Staff Headcount is based on a company’s global headcount, not only Irish operations

We aim to promote a high degree of competitiveness, productivity and internationalisation in the digitalisation and sustainable growth of manufacturing SMEs, contributing to the economic growth of Ireland. IDEAM assists manufacturing SMEs in digital transformation and our core goal is to unlock new opportunities for industry by working collaboratively.

content.heading: Supporting Irish Manufacturing Excellence

content.heading: About Smarter Factory Technology Gateway
content.text: The Smarter Factory Technology Gateway is an Irish Government-EU co-funded industry-facing organisation within the Technological University of the Shannon (TUS) and IDEAM Research Institute. We support Irish manufacturers across all industries including pharmaceuticals, life sciences, food, beverage, electronics, discrete manufacturing, and many others. Part of the Enterprise Ireland Technology Gateway Network
The Smarter Factory Technology Gateway Programme is co-financed by the Government of Ireland and the European Union through the ERDF Southern, Eastern & Midland Regional Programme 2021-2027.
The Smarter Factory provides comprehensive industry support through three main approaches

We provide customized training modules for manufacturers’ employees, enabling upskilling through collaborative ‘on-the-project’ research with our team. Multiple automation interconnected systems mimic real-world scenarios for comprehensive testing.
The Smarter Factory Technology Gateway Programme is co-financed by the Government of Ireland and the European Union through the ERDF Southern, Eastern & Midland Regional Programme 2021-2027.

For technology companies, collaboration with us provides a beachhead ‘land and expand’ opportunity to showcase solutions in real manufacturing environments.

content.heading: 1.Research Projects in the company.
content.text: Collaborate with us on tailored research that tackles real challenges in your company.

content.heading: 2.Digital Hub & Researcher access for the company
content.text: Gain hands-on access to our digital hub and work directly with experienced researchers.

content.heading: 3.Blend of custom testing, training, certification, validation & upskilling
content.text: From testing and certification to training, validation, and staff upskilling, we design solutions around your needs.

content.heading: 4.Masterclasses & Specialist training.
content.text: Learn through a blend of broad masterclasses and in-depth specialist training, ensuring your team has the right skills for the future.

content.heading: Jim O’Hagan, Smarter Factory Gateway Manager
content.text: TUS Coonagh Engineering Campus

content.heading: Jim O’Hagan, Smarter Factory Gateway Manager
content.text: LinkedIn: Smarter Factory Technology Gateway

content.heading: Smarter Factory Core Expertise
content.text: The Smarter Factory Technology Gateway, in close collaboration with Enterprise Ireland and industry partners across the manufacturing value chain, focuses on research projects that drive impactful innovation.

content.heading: Smarter Factory Core Expertise
content.text: Central to these partnerships is the belief in the power of data. By harnessing cyber-physical systems (CPS), the Internet of Things (IoT), and edge devices, the Gateway enables the development of intelligent systems, cognitive robotics, computer vision, and artificial intelligence (AI).

content.heading: Smarter Factory Core Expertise
content.text: Researchers at the University bring deep expertise in areas such as industrial automation, control systems, machine learning, digital twins, algorithm development, and industrial data management.

content.heading: Smarter Factory Core Expertise
content.text: A key priority is demonstrating the practical application of these advanced technologies in real industrial settings, helping companies improve product quality, predictive maintenance, efficiency, capacity, cost savings, and sustainability.

content.heading: Smarter Factory Core Expertise
content.text: Guided by an Industry Advisory Board and regional stakeholders, the Gateway supports businesses in navigating Industry 4.0 and 5.0, adopting digital manufacturing technologies, and accessing opportunities through national and European research programmes.

content.heading: Smarter Factory Core Expertise
content.text: Supporting Operational Excellence and Data-driven Decision making
The Smarter Factory Technology Gateway Programme is co-financed by the Government of Ireland and the European Union through the ERDF Southern, Eastern & Midland Regional Programme 2021-2027.
The Smarter Factory Technology Gateway Programme is co-financed by the Government of Ireland and the European Union through the ERDF Southern, Eastern & Midland Regional Programme 2021-2027.

content.heading: Smarter Factory Technology Gateway
content.text: The Smarter Factory Technology Gateway is part of Ireland’s national network of Technology Gateways, operating as one of six members within the Applied IoT cluster. We are an industry-facing research organization embedded within the Technological University of the Shannon (TUS) and the IDEAM Research Institute.
The Smarter Factory Technology Gateway Programme is co-financed by the Government of Ireland and the European Union through the ERDF Southern, Eastern & Midland Regional Programme 2021-2027.

content.heading: AIDEA - Artificial Intelligence for a new Design Education Approach
content.text: Short project description: AIDEA aims to increase the quality of European Design Education and its alignment with the market needs by innovating training curricula, strengthening design schools and academies by incorporating the 3 different meanings of “Intelligence”: The Intelligence of the hand, Artificial Intelligence and Social Intelligence. It will do so by enabling teachers to empower students to harness the transformative potential of AI in design while fostering critical thinking, creativity, and ethical awareness.

content.heading: Skills4Circularity - Upskilling and reskilling the textile industry sector to face the challenges in recycling, traceability and certification of recycled products
content.text: Skills4Circularity addresses critical skills shortages in the European textile industry by integrating circular economy principles, digital transformation, and sustainability into vocational and higher education. The project will develop a comprehensive training ecosystem equipping the workforce with future-proof skills through innovative, modular programmes including MOOCs, regional and international bootcamps, and interregional hackathons.

Supporting Europe’s digital and green transition, DigiWind will deliver the interdisciplinary Specialised Education Programmes (SEP) needed to future-proof the careers of Science, Technology, Engineering and Math (STEM) professionals in wind and energy systems through their acquisition of advanced digital skills including the Digital Europe Programme’s (DEP) key capacity areas of High-Performance Computing (HPC), Artificial Intelligence (AI), Cybersecurity, and other emerging technologies.
Our website address is: https://ideam.ie.

content.heading: What personal data we collect and why we collect it
content.text: When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

content.heading: What personal data we collect and why we collect it
content.text: An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

content.heading: What personal data we collect and why we collect it
content.text: If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

content.heading: What personal data we collect and why we collect it
content.text: If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

content.heading: What personal data we collect and why we collect it
content.text: If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

content.heading: What personal data we collect and why we collect it
content.text: When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select “Remember Me”, your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

content.heading: What personal data we collect and why we collect it
content.text: If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

content.heading: Embedded content from other websites
content.text: Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

content.heading: Embedded content from other websites
content.text: These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

content.heading: Embedded content from other websites
content.text: If you request a password reset, your IP address will be included in the reset email.

content.heading: Embedded content from other websites
content.text: If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

content.heading: Embedded content from other websites
content.text: For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

content.heading: What rights you have over your data
content.text: If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

content.heading: What rights you have over your data
content.text: Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

content.heading: Digi Manufacturing 2.0 aims to create and enhance new cross-border strategic value chains and enhance the green uptake between both Manufacturing NI and IDEAM Cluster manufacturing SMEs.
content.text: Digi Manufacturing aims to establish collaborative innovation projects between members of the IDEAM Cluster and Manufacturing NI fostering the collective digital and green transition of their members.

content.heading: Cross-Border Cluster Collaboration to foster new Strategic Value Chains and Enhance the Digitisation and Green Transition uptake of Manufacturing SMEs to become more competitive
content.text: Digi Manufacturing aims to establish collaborative innovation projects between members of the IDEAM Cluster and Manufacturing NI fostering the collective digital and green transition of their members.
